contentBlocks.original:
नारायणेति वाग्वल्वी चतुरक्षर पल्लव ।
धर्मार्थ काममोक्षाश्च परदानार्थ संशयाः ।।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇeti vāgvalvī caturakṣara pallava .
dharmārtha kāmamokṣāśca paradānārtha saṃśayāḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: The four letters 'Na', 'Ra,'Ya', 'Na' are the four shoots on the creeper of speech and are undoubtedly indicative of the four Purusharthas- Dharma, Artha, Kaama and Moksha.
contentBlocks.meanings: नारायणेति (nārāyaṇeti) – as 'Nārāyaṇa' (nārāyaṇa:Nārāyaṇa | iti:as); वाग्वल्वी (vāgvalvī) – creeper of speech (vāk:speech | valvī:creeper); चतुरक्षर (caturakṣara) – four-lettered (catur:four | akṣara:letter); पल्लव (pallava) – shoot; धर्मार्थ (dharmārtha) – Dharma and Artha (dharma:righteousness | artha:wealth); काममोक्षाश्च (kāmamokṣāśca) – and Kāma and Moksha (kāma:desire | mokṣa:liberation | ca:and); परदानार्थ (paradānārtha) – for the purpose of giving (para:for | dāna:giving | artha:purpose); संशयाः (saṃśayāḥ) – doubts;
